name: Bernard Herrmann
abstract: Bernard Herrmann (born Maximillian Herman; June 29, 1911 – December 24, 1975) was an American composer and conductor best known for his work in film scoring. He is widely regarded as one of the greatest film composers of all time.

works_created.t: Organ Grinder's Swing arranged in an 18th Century Manner
The Day the Earth Stood Still
North by Northwest, Concert Etude for Piano
Suite from The Devil and Daniel Webster

about_quotes.speaker: Howie Shear
about_quotes.evidence: I think Bernard Herrmann, who did Psycho... And he really changed the way, especially strings are scored in films.
about_quotes.doc_title: Howie Shear interviewed by Monk Rowe, Dunkirk, New York, October 11, 2014

about_quotes.speaker: Bobby Shew
about_quotes.evidence: If they say Bernard Herrmann, you go ohhhh shit... Because Bernard Herrmann’s going to be a pain in the ass. He’s going to be — he’s like Hitler you know? Real militant and screaming and hollering and things like that.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bobby Shew interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 1, 2020
